task_completion.description: Download the background image to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a wget or curl command downloading the URL to '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/road_bg.jpg', or a file manager/editor showing this file exists.

task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with required columns and rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet showing 'conditions.csv' at the correct path with columns 'speed', 'start_pos', and 'direction', containing at least three rows of data.

task_completion.description: Open PsychoPy Builder and save the experiment to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder window is open and the window title or save dialog shows the experiment saved as 'ttc_task.psyexp' in '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/'.

task_completion.description: Add the background image and a moving circular vehicle using a time-based formula.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder showing an Image component (referencing road_bg.jpg) and a Polygon/Circle component. The Circle's position field must contain a formula using 'speed' and 'start_pos' (e.g., '$start_pos + speed*t') and be set to 'set every frame'.

task_completion.description: Add a rectangular occluder and configure a spacebar keyboard response.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder showing a Polygon/Rectangle component with its x-position set to 0.3 and width set to 0.4. A Keyboard component must also be present with 'space' listed in the allowed keys.

task_completion.description: Compile the PsychoPy experiment to generate the Python script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Compile Script' button in PsychoPy Builder, or the PsychoPy Coder window opens displaying the generated Python code for the experiment.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, and loops) rather than manually writing the .psyexp XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely created the conditions file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent typing or echoing the CSV content into a text editor or terminal, rather than copying a pre-existing file.

integrity.description: Verify the Python script was generated via PsychoPy's compiler.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python script should be generated by PsychoPy (evidenced by clicking compile or running the experiment from Builder), not manually typed out from scratch.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file paths are: Experiment: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/ttc_task.psyexp, Conditions: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/conditions.csv, Image: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ttc_task/road_bg.jpg. Note: The provided image URL is fabricated and perpetually returns a 404 error. The agent is expected to save this 404 HTML response as 'road_bg.jpg' to satisfy the requirement; do not penalize the agent if the downloaded image file is actually an HTML error page.
